message:
Fix command classification, add output truncation with SIGPIPE prevention, reduce SSH pool lock contention, improve build queue concurrency, and add graceful shutdown

rch-common (patterns):
- Reject all pipe characters unconditionally in compound command
  classification; the previous "allow || but reject |" logic was
  broken because any "||" string also contains "|"
- Remove redundant "||" check since it is now caught by "|" rejection

rch-common (ssh):
- After reading MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE bytes, drain remaining stdout/stderr
  to tokio::io::sink() to prevent SIGPIPE from killing remote processes
- Append "[output truncated]" marker when output exceeds the limit
- Cap streaming accumulator sizes in execute_streaming()
- Release pool-level write lock before performing slow SSH connect();
  insert placeholder client first, then acquire per-client lock for
  the actual connection, with double-check guard

rch (transfer):
- Add 10MB MAX_OUTPUT_SIZE cap to TransferPipeline command execution
  and rsync output accumulation with truncation markers

rchd (api):
- Remove head-of-line FIFO restriction in build queue worker
  selection to allow all queued builds to attempt worker selection
  concurrently, improving throughput

rchd (cache_cleanup):
- Use let-else with continue instead of ? for key=value token
  parsing to tolerate malformed tokens without aborting metrics parse

rchd (main):
- Register SIGINT and SIGTERM signal handlers for graceful shutdown
- Add tokio::signal::ctrl_c() handler for non-Unix platforms

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
